# writing-showcase-readmes

This repository contains one Codex skill: `writing-showcase-readmes`.

Purpose: create, rewrite, or polish GitHub README files for repositories, agent skills, CLIs, libraries, apps, and open source launches. The skill focuses on rich but honest README elements: badges, small icons, navigation rows, proof tables, Star History charts, compatibility matrices, quickstarts, docs tables, comparison tables, community links, and collapsible details.

Install for Codex:

```bash
mkdir -p ~/.codex/skills
cp -R skills/writing-showcase-readmes ~/.codex/skills/
```

Windows PowerShell:

```powershell
New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force "$env:USERPROFILE\.codex\skills" | Out-Null
Copy-Item -Recurse -Force ".\skills\writing-showcase-readmes" "$env:USERPROFILE\.codex\skills\writing-showcase-readmes"
```

Invoke:

```text
Use $writing-showcase-readmes to rewrite this repository README into a polished showcase.
```

Important files:

- `skills/writing-showcase-readmes/SKILL.md`: core workflow.
- `skills/writing-showcase-readmes/references/readme-elements.md`: README element library and snippets.
- `skills/writing-showcase-readmes/agents/openai.yaml`: Codex UI metadata.
- `scripts/validate_skill.py`: local/CI validator.

Integrity rule: the skill must not invent metrics, screenshots, CI status, package names, adoption numbers, benchmarks, compatibility, or governance claims.
